description: SkillWeave SAD Dependency DAG — encodes the Directed Acyclic Graph of skill dependencies. Used by the skillweave-orchestrator to topologically sort a multi-skill plan so skills execute in the correct order, with their prerequisites satisfied.

edge_types.required: The target skill MUST be executed after the source skill; source output is consumed by target
edge_types.alternative: The target skill is an alternative execution path to another target (either-or)
edge_types.concurrent: The target skill can run alongside other skills with the same source
edge_types.optional: The target skill is a recommended but optional follow-up

edges.note: Always proceed from brainstorming to writing-plans. Do not implement before planning.
Use executing-plans for sequential, tightly-coupled tasks
Use subagent-driven-development for mostly-independent tasks that benefit from parallel execution
Each task in the plan uses TDD; these run concurrently per-task
Code review after implementation is recommended before verification
After all TDD cycles complete, run full verification
Verify the entire implementation after all subagents complete
After finding root cause, fix using TDD to prevent regression
After review is produced, process the feedback
Browser test evidence is part of final verification
Use worktrees when working on multiple features simultaneously
Worktrees provide clean isolation for subagent execution

standard_feature.description: Full feature development from idea to merge

bug_fix.description: Debug and fix a failing test or unexpected behavior

parallel_research.description: Gather information from multiple sources concurrently
